title: Birds and Dinosaurs
alt: Sure, T. rex is closer in height to Stegosaurus than a sparrow. But that doesn't tell you much; 'Dinosaur Comics' author Ryan North is closer in height to certain dinosaurs than to the average human.
transcript:
By any reasonable definition, T. rex is more closely related to sparrows than to Stegosaurus.
[Diagram showing that Stegosaurus came earlier than T. rex, along with it showing that T. rex came closer in time to sparrows. Evaluation criteria "separation by time", "phylogenetic distance" and "physical similarity" are highlighted in red.]
Birds aren't descended from dinosaurs, they are dinosaurs.
Which means that the fastest animal alive today is a small carnivorous dinosaur, Falco peregrinus.
[A picture of two birds is shown, one large and one small.]
It preys mainly on other dinosaurs, which it strikes and kills in midair with its claws.
[In red:] This is a good world.
explanation:
At the time the comic was released s were commonly considered to be a separate  of . However, this classification is false according to . Taking into account that birds developed around  out of small  dinosaurs, birds along with crocodiles are indeed the remaining representatives of the . This premise appeared also in comic 867: Herpetology.

This relation between birds and dinosaurs is depicted in the comic in a  which shows that  is more closely related to the common  than to . Not only do the former share a phylogenetic branch, but T. rex also lived around 80 million years after Stegosaurus. The concurrence of both species in popular culture is a widespread error. T. rex is also much more alike to modern birds than to other dinosaurs in terms of anatomy. This relationship was pointed out on the Science journal the week of the comic.

The comic draws the conclusion that if birds must in fact be considered modern dinosaurs, the hunting practice of birds of prey (specifically, the ) is consequently a dinosaur fight. For an inveterate (but not invertebrate) dinosaur aficionado like Randall, this fact must make the modern world much more attractive.

The title text alludes to the webcomic Dinosaur Comics author , who says that he stands 2 m tall (exactly 2m is just slightly under 6'6¾"). For comparison, a T-Rex could be twice as high as Ryan, to the top of their hip/level back, and an average unladen African sparrow (as with some of the smaller dinosaurs) is perhaps just 16cm (~6") beak-to-tail. At that page, the title text of the comic strip from the same day refers to Randall and xkcd. In reality, unless someone actually is exactly average height, they could always find examples of dinosaurs with heights that are closer to their own than to the 'average' person.

White Hat and Megan (even combined) are definitely shorter than T. Rex.

The conclusion of this comic is referenced in the title text of the last image in the Plastic Dinosaurs What if?

Xkcd first acknowledged Dinosaur Comics in 145: Parody Week: Dinosaur Comics.
